question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Summary - Your symptom (incoming calls from one specific new number block are not delivered at all; callers hear busy tone or the call drops after a few seconds; nothing appears in Teams Admin Center) matches a known Direct Routing / SBC translation issue we have seen before
context: SOURCE 8: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Incoming calls from specific numbers not received Ticket number: TI-1612036 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 22 Owner: Employee 41 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 18.09.25 08:56 Closure Closure date: 29.09.25 16:18 Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing and translation rules adjusted according to Direct Routing Blueprint. Incoming calls fully functional again. Activities 18.09.25 08:40 – Email from Contact Person 22 Good morning We have noticed that incoming calls from certain customers are no longer reaching us. Callers report that they either receive a busy tone or the connection is disconnected after a few seconds. Other external calls are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 18.09.25 08:56 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 41. 18.09.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Could you please provide the following information: – Example phone numbers where the issue occurs – Since when the issue has existed – Whether all users are affected or only specific ones Thank you. 18.09.25 10:15 – Customer response Here are two examples: +41 44 785 23 10 +41 44 785 23 11 Other numbers such as +41 31 … work without issue. The problem has existed since last week. All employees are affected. 18.09.25 11:40 – Internal analysis Test call from private mobile (different number range) → works. Test with number in specified range → call not delivered. In the Teams Admin Center, no incoming call is shown. 19.09.25 09:05 – Initial hypothesis Possible provider issue. Carrier contacted. 19.09.25 14:20 – Carrier feedback From the carrier’s perspective, the call is correctly delivered to the company’s SBC. No routing errors visible on their side. 22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis For functioning numbers: INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams. For affected numbers: INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”. 22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer Good day We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC. However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally. Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions? Kind regards 22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers. The affected numbers belong to this new range. 23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review Comparison with document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3” Section regarding: • Normalization of incoming numbers • E.164 format • Translation rules on SBC • Voice routing policies Finding: New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table. Routing rule covers only the old number range. 24.09.25 – Test Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range. Test call performed. Call correctly signaled and delivered. Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible. 25.09.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated. Internal documentation had not yet been updated. 26.09.25 – Adjustment Routing and translation rules extended according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 29.09.25 – Customer feedback All tested numbers are working again. Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls. Thank you for the support. 29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 22 The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Short answer: this should be handled as a 2nd‑level (specialist/UC/Direct Routing) ticket — not a pure 1st‑level desk fix. Reasoning and recommended triage steps are below. Why this is 2nd-level - In the matching case the carrier confirmed calls were delivered to the customer SBC, but the SBC either returned “404 Not Found” for the affected numbers or did not forward the calls internally — i.e. the problem was in SBC translation/routing, not the carrier nor the Teams client
context: SOURCE 8: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Incoming calls from specific numbers not received Ticket number: TI-1612036 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 22 Owner: Employee 41 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 18.09.25 08:56 Closure Closure date: 29.09.25 16:18 Closure text: Analysis completed. Kind regards 18.09.25 08:56 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 41. 18.09.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Could you please provide the following information: – Example phone numbers where the issue occurs – Since when the issue has existed – Whether all users are affected or only specific ones Thank you. 18.09.25 10:15 – Customer response Here are two examples: +41 44 785 23 10 +41 44 785 23 11 Other numbers such as +41 31 … work without issue. The problem has existed since last week. All employees are affected. 18.09.25 11:40 – Internal analysis Test call from private mobile (different number range) → works. Test with number in specified range → call not delivered. In the Teams Admin Center, no incoming call is shown. 19.09.25 09:05 – Initial hypothesis Possible provider issue. Carrier contacted. 19.09.25 14:20 – Carrier feedback From the carrier’s perspective, the call is correctly delivered to the company’s SBC. No routing errors visible on their side. 22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis For functioning numbers: INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams. For affected numbers: INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”. 22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer Good day We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC. However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally. Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions? Kind regards 22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers. Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible. 25.09.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated. Internal documentation had not yet been updated. 26.09.25 – Adjustment Routing and translation rules extended according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 29.09.25 – Customer feedback All tested numbers are working again. Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls. Thank you for the support. 29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 22 The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Similar incidents required changes to voice routing / SBC translation tables, or changes to infrastructure (session timers, NAT, firewall rules) and were resolved by the UC/Direct‑Routing team following the internal routing/Direct‑Routing blueprints

Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 9: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External number cannot be dialed Ticket number: TI-1609823 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 21 Owner: Employee 39 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 03.09.25 09:08 Closure Closure date: 12.09.25 15:40 Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing configuration adjusted according to Teams Voice Architecture. Dialing external numbers possible again. Activities 03.09.25 08:55 – Email from Contact Person 21 Good morning Some employees have been unable to dial external phone numbers since today. Internal Teams calls function normally. External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears. Could you please check this? Kind regards 03.09.25 09:08 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 39. 03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Are all users affected or only specific ones? Does it affect all external numbers or only certain ones? Were there any recent changes to licenses or user roles? Thank you for your feedback. 03.09.25 10:25 – Customer response It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team. Other teams can call normally. It affects all external numbers. We are not aware of any license changes. 03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. Internal call → works. External call → immediate termination after dialing. Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”. 04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer Good morning We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration. Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group? Kind regards 04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week. New Azure AD groups were created for this purpose. 04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis Users have: • Teams license • Calling Plan not active • Direct Routing configured Observation: Voice Routing Policy = “Global” Not “DR-Sales” Comparison with working user from another team shows: Voice Routing Policy = “DR-Standard” 05.09.25 09:05 – Internal review Get-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy shows that policy “Global” does not reference any PSTN Usage. Policy “DR-Standard” includes PSTN Usage “External-Route”. 05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer Good day We have identified that the affected users are currently assigned a different Voice Routing Policy than the other employees. We are reviewing why this assignment was changed. Kind regards 08.09.25 – Internal analysis New Azure AD group “Enterprise-Sales” was created. Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group. The internal document “Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3” states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy. 09.09.25 – Test phase Temporary assignment of policy “DR-Standard” to affected user. Test call to mobile phone performed. Call successful. Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible. 10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team Provisioning script was modified two weeks ago. New group structure was not reflected in the routing blueprint. 11.09.25 – Adjustment Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture. Provisioning logic adjusted. 12.09.25 – Customer feedback Good day All affected users can dial external numbers again. Longer calls also function normally. Thank you for your support. 12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 21 The root cause was an incorrect Voice Routing Policy assignment following an organizational restructuring. The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture. External calls are now possible again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 16: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds Ticket number: TI-1604879 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 16 Owner: Employee 35 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 22.07.25 09:14 Closure Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52 Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable. Activities 22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk Good morning Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds. The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time. Internal Teams calls are not affected. Could you please review this? Kind regards 22.07.25 09:14 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35. 22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Thank you for your report. Could you please confirm: – Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls? – Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration? – Are all locations affected or only a specific one? Thank you very much. 22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response It only affects the Basel location. Both incoming and outgoing calls. The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier. Home office users are not affected. 22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. External call to mobile phone. Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds. No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.” 23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis Possible client error or unstable connection. Test with another device → same behavior. Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change. 23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs. Signaling is established correctly. The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client. Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location? Kind regards 23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented. A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created. Could this be relevant? 24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis SBC logs reviewed: – SIP INVITE successful – 200 OK correct – Call established – After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC No obvious error code. 24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis No blocking visible. Ports open according to standard configuration. 24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis Session-Expires header reviewed. Session timer set to 30 seconds. Comparison with other locations shows: There, 180 seconds. 25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation Document: “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1” Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant. Note in the blueprint: For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination. 25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC. Test call performed. Call remains stable for several minutes. Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible. 28.07.25 – Coordination with network team New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy. UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds. Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds. 29.07.25 – Adjustment NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 30.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We conducted several test calls. No more disconnections observed. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you very much. 31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 16 The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Calls now remain stable. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 5: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls Ticket number: TI-1602241 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 14 Owner: Employee 33 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 08.07.25 08:21 Closure Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05 Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again. Activities 08.07.25 08:10 Call from Mr. Contact Person 14. He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”. Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected. 08.07.25 08:21 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33. 08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14 Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14 Thank you for your report. Could you please briefly confirm the following points: – Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)? – Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected? – Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones? – Since when has the issue existed? Thank you for your feedback. Kind regards 08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14 Good morning Yes, it affects only external calls. Both incoming and outgoing. At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location. Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday. Kind regards 08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note Test call performed with user S. Meier. Internal Teams call works. External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing. Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message. 08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test. Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical? 08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response We tested with three additional persons. Same behavior everywhere. Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly). LAN connection. Home office users do not seem to be affected. 09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again Good morning We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend. Could this have an impact? Kind regards 09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note Firewall change timing plausible. Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team. 09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18 09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration. Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established. We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream). We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings. Kind regards 10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis SBC shows successful SIP connections. No call drops. Media negotiation (SDP) is performed. Notable: RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction. 10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison New rule implemented at Zurich location. Outbound traffic configured more restrictively. Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges. 10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports. Test call performed. Audio immediately available in both directions. Firewall reverted to restrictive rule. Issue occurs again. 11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer Good day We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location. Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked. We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification. We will provide an update shortly. Kind regards 14.07.25 – Internal documentation review Comparison with internal document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1” Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled. Finding: Zurich location configured with Media Bypass. Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range. 16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 17.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We tested several external calls. Audio works normally again. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you for your support. Kind regards 18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 14 The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment. The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification. Voice transmission is now stable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . These actions typically require privileged access to SBCs, voice routing policies and coordination with network/identity teams — beyond normal 1st‑level scope. Immediate triage checklist (1st‑level can perform before escalation) 1. Collect example numbers in the affected range (give at least two numbers) and exact timestamps of failed calls — this was requested and used in the prior ticket to reproduce the issue

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - If changes were made recently to provisioning, Azure AD groups or routing policy assignment, validate policy assignments (other tickets show problems where group/provisioning changes or wrong voice routing policy caused related call problems)
context: SOURCE 9: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External number cannot be dialed Ticket number: TI-1609823 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 21 Owner: Employee 39 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 03.09.25 09:08 Closure Closure date: 12.09.25 15:40 Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing configuration adjusted according to Teams Voice Architecture. Dialing external numbers possible again. Activities 03.09.25 08:55 – Email from Contact Person 21 Good morning Some employees have been unable to dial external phone numbers since today. Internal Teams calls function normally. External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears. Could you please check this? Kind regards 03.09.25 09:08 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 39. 03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Are all users affected or only specific ones? Does it affect all external numbers or only certain ones? Were there any recent changes to licenses or user roles? Thank you for your feedback. 03.09.25 10:25 – Customer response It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team. Other teams can call normally. It affects all external numbers. We are not aware of any license changes. 03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. Internal call → works. External call → immediate termination after dialing. Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”. 04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer Good morning We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration. Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group? Kind regards 04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week. New Azure AD groups were created for this purpose. 04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis Users have: • Teams license • Calling Plan not active • Direct Routing configured Observation: Voice Routing Policy = “Global” Not “DR-Sales” Comparison with working user from another team shows: Voice Routing Policy = “DR-Standard” 05.09.25 09:05 – Internal review Get-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy shows that policy “Global” does not reference any PSTN Usage. Policy “DR-Standard” includes PSTN Usage “External-Route”. 05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer Good day We have identified that the affected users are currently assigned a different Voice Routing Policy than the other employees. We are reviewing why this assignment was changed. Kind regards 08.09.25 – Internal analysis New Azure AD group “Enterprise-Sales” was created. Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group. The internal document “Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3” states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy. 09.09.25 – Test phase Temporary assignment of policy “DR-Standard” to affected user. Test call to mobile phone performed. Call successful. Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible. 10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team Provisioning script was modified two weeks ago. New group structure was not reflected in the routing blueprint. 11.09.25 – Adjustment Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture. Provisioning logic adjusted. 12.09.25 – Customer feedback Good day All affected users can dial external numbers again. Longer calls also function normally. Thank you for your support. 12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 21 The root cause was an incorrect Voice Routing Policy assignment following an organizational restructuring. The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture. External calls are now possible again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - If the call is dropping after a few seconds rather than never delivered, also check session timers, NAT and firewall timeouts (we have seen 30s session/NAT timeouts and media/firewall port restrictions cause related problems)
context: SOURCE 16: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds Ticket number: TI-1604879 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 16 Owner: Employee 35 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 22.07.25 09:14 Closure Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52 Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable. Activities 22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk Good morning Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds. The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time. Internal Teams calls are not affected. Could you please review this? Kind regards 22.07.25 09:14 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35. 22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Thank you for your report. Could you please confirm: – Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls? – Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration? – Are all locations affected or only a specific one? Thank you very much. 22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response It only affects the Basel location. Both incoming and outgoing calls. The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier. Home office users are not affected. 22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. External call to mobile phone. Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds. No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.” 23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis Possible client error or unstable connection. Test with another device → same behavior. Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change. 23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs. Signaling is established correctly. The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client. Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location? Kind regards 23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented. A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created. Could this be relevant? 24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis SBC logs reviewed: – SIP INVITE successful – 200 OK correct – Call established – After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC No obvious error code. 24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis No blocking visible. Ports open according to standard configuration. 24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis Session-Expires header reviewed. Session timer set to 30 seconds. Comparison with other locations shows: There, 180 seconds. 25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation Document: “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1” Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant. Note in the blueprint: For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination. 25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC. Test call performed. Call remains stable for several minutes. Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible. 28.07.25 – Coordination with network team New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy. UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds. Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds. 29.07.25 – Adjustment NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 30.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We conducted several test calls. No more disconnections observed. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you very much. 31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 16 The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Calls now remain stable. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 5: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls Ticket number: TI-1602241 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 14 Owner: Employee 33 Origin: Phone Type: Incident Created on: 08.07.25 08:21 Closure Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05 Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again. Activities 08.07.25 08:10 Call from Mr. Contact Person 14. He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”. Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected. 08.07.25 08:21 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33. 08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14 Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14 Thank you for your report. Could you please briefly confirm the following points: – Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)? – Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected? – Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones? – Since when has the issue existed? Thank you for your feedback. Kind regards 08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14 Good morning Yes, it affects only external calls. Both incoming and outgoing. At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location. Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday. Kind regards 08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note Test call performed with user S. Meier. Internal Teams call works. External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing. Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message. 08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test. Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical? 08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response We tested with three additional persons. Same behavior everywhere. Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly). LAN connection. Home office users do not seem to be affected. 09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again Good morning We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend. Could this have an impact? Kind regards 09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note Firewall change timing plausible. Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team. 09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18 09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer Good day We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration. Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established. We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream). We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings. Kind regards 10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis SBC shows successful SIP connections. No call drops. Media negotiation (SDP) is performed. Notable: RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction. 10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison New rule implemented at Zurich location. Outbound traffic configured more restrictively. Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges. 10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports. Test call performed. Audio immediately available in both directions. Firewall reverted to restrictive rule. Issue occurs again. 11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer Good day We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location. Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked. We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification. We will provide an update shortly. Kind regards 14.07.25 – Internal documentation review Comparison with internal document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1” Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled. Finding: Zurich location configured with Media Bypass. Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range. 16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 17.07.25 – Customer feedback Good morning We tested several external calls. Audio works normally again. Longer conversations are also stable. Thank you for your support. Kind regards 18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 14 The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment. The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification. Voice transmission is now stable again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

answer: . Suggested ticket classification and priority - Classification: 2nd‑level / UC / Direct Routing / SBC specialist (requires access to SBC logs and routing/translation configuration). This classification is supported by the resolution steps in the comparable incident
context: SOURCE 8: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Incoming calls from specific numbers not received Ticket number: TI-1612036 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 22 Owner: Employee 41 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 18.09.25 08:56 Closure Closure date: 29.09.25 16:18 Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing and translation rules adjusted according to Direct Routing Blueprint. Incoming calls fully functional again. Activities 18.09.25 08:40 – Email from Contact Person 22 Good morning We have noticed that incoming calls from certain customers are no longer reaching us. Callers report that they either receive a busy tone or the connection is disconnected after a few seconds. Other external calls are functioning normally. Could you please check this? Kind regards 18.09.25 08:56 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 41. 18.09.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Could you please provide the following information: – Example phone numbers where the issue occurs – Since when the issue has existed – Whether all users are affected or only specific ones Thank you. 18.09.25 10:15 – Customer response Here are two examples: +41 44 785 23 10 +41 44 785 23 11 Other numbers such as +41 31 … work without issue. The problem has existed since last week. All employees are affected. 18.09.25 11:40 – Internal analysis Test call from private mobile (different number range) → works. Test with number in specified range → call not delivered. In the Teams Admin Center, no incoming call is shown. 19.09.25 09:05 – Initial hypothesis Possible provider issue. Carrier contacted. 19.09.25 14:20 – Carrier feedback From the carrier’s perspective, the call is correctly delivered to the company’s SBC. No routing errors visible on their side. 22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis For functioning numbers: INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams. For affected numbers: INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”. 22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer Good day We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC. However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally. Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions? Kind regards 22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers. The affected numbers belong to this new range. 23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review Comparison with document “Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3” Section regarding: • Normalization of incoming numbers • E.164 format • Translation rules on SBC • Voice routing policies Finding: New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table. Routing rule covers only the old number range. 24.09.25 – Test Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range. Test call performed. Call correctly signaled and delivered. Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible. 25.09.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated. Internal documentation had not yet been updated. 26.09.25 – Adjustment Routing and translation rules extended according to architectural specification. Details not documented in ticket for security reasons. 29.09.25 – Customer feedback All tested numbers are working again. Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls. Thank you for the support. 29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 22 The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range. The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture. Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company SOURCE 9: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External number cannot be dialed Ticket number: TI-1609823 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 21 Owner: Employee 39 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 03.09.25 09:08 Closure Closure date: 12.09.25 15:40 Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing configuration adjusted according to Teams Voice Architecture. Dialing external numbers possible again. Activities 03.09.25 08:55 – Email from Contact Person 21 Good morning Some employees have been unable to dial external phone numbers since today. Internal Teams calls function normally. External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears. Could you please check this? Kind regards 03.09.25 09:08 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 39. 03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Are all users affected or only specific ones? Does it affect all external numbers or only certain ones? Were there any recent changes to licenses or user roles? Thank you for your feedback. 03.09.25 10:25 – Customer response It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team. Other teams can call normally. It affects all external numbers. We are not aware of any license changes. 03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. Internal call → works. External call → immediate termination after dialing. Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”. 04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer Good morning We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration. Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group? Kind regards 04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week. New Azure AD groups were created for this purpose. 04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis Users have: • Teams license • Calling Plan not active • Direct Routing configured Observation: Voice Routing Policy = “Global” Not “DR-Sales” Comparison with working user from another team shows: Voice Routing Policy = “DR-Standard” 05.09.25 09:05 – Internal review Get-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy shows that policy “Global” does not reference any PSTN Usage. Policy “DR-Standard” includes PSTN Usage “External-Route”. 05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer Good day We have identified that the affected users are currently assigned a different Voice Routing Policy than the other employees. We are reviewing why this assignment was changed. Kind regards 08.09.25 – Internal analysis New Azure AD group “Enterprise-Sales” was created. Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group. The internal document “Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3” states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy. 09.09.25 – Test phase Temporary assignment of policy “DR-Standard” to affected user. Test call to mobile phone performed. Call successful. Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible. 10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team Provisioning script was modified two weeks ago. New group structure was not reflected in the routing blueprint. 11.09.25 – Adjustment Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture. Provisioning logic adjusted. 12.09.25 – Customer feedback Good day All affected users can dial external numbers again. Longer calls also function normally. Thank you for your support. 12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 21 The root cause was an incorrect Voice Routing Policy assignment following an organizational restructuring. The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture. External calls are now possible again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Priority: Escalate to high if the number block is customer‑facing and many callers are affected; include customer impact (number of affected callers/users) and whether business‑critical numbers are in the block. What to include when you escalate (copy into the 2nd‑level ticket) - Example affected numbers (2+), sample timestamps, any SIP traces from the carrier, screenshots/logs showing no incoming call in Teams Admin Center, description of recent changes (provider delivered new number range; numbers migrated) — these items were actionable in the referenced incident
